source.multipart_basis: Decomposition mirrors a Cal Poly CDS Rev 14 3U CubeSat primary structure assembled from four deployer-rail corner members, +/-X and +/-Y side panels, +/-Z deck plates, internal PC/104 stack rods, and payload/ADCS mounting trays, typical of UKSEDS Satellite Design Challenge entries.

full_prompt:
Design a 3U CubeSat primary structure benchmark compliant with Cal Poly CubeSat Design Specification (CDS) Rev 14 for 3U deployers. The structure is a monolithic 6061-T6 aluminum skeletonized chassis with external envelope 100.0 +/- 0.1 mm x 100.0 +/- 0.1 mm x 340.5 +/- 0.3 mm (stacked 3U), four deployer rails on the long corners (8.5 x 8.5 mm rails, at least 85% of each 340.5 mm long rail in contact with the P-POD), and a total mass at or below 4.0 kg (3 x 1.33 kg) per CDS Rev 14 3U mass cap. The structure must support internal electronics stacks using the standard PC/104 form factor (90 x 96 mm boards, 4 mounting holes at 85.60 x 82.55 mm), spaced up to 12 boards along the long axis. Loads per GEVS-SE GSFC-STD-7000 typical qualification levels for a secondary payload on a launcher like Falcon 9 / Vega / PSLV: (LC1) quasi-static 12 g axial + 7 g lateral simultaneously, (LC2) sine vibration 2.5 g at 5-100 Hz, (LC3) random vibration GRMS 14.1 at 20-2000 Hz, (LC4) shock response 1000 g peak 100-10000 Hz. Material 6061-T6 (yield 276 MPa, ultimate 310 MPa, E 69 GPa, density 2700 kg/m^3). Pass/fail: (R1) quasi-static max von Mises stress must not exceed 137 MPa (yield / SF 2.0 per NASA-STD-5001B Category A bracket), (R2) first natural frequency must be at least 100 Hz to clear the launcher primary interface per ECSS-E-HB-32-26A, (R3) total structure mass at or below 4.0 kg, (R4) a linear buckling analysis under 12 g axial + 7 g lateral must yield a first-mode load factor of at least 2.0, and (R5) deployer rail flatness tolerance must be +/- 0.1 mm along the 340.5 mm rail length. Vibration analysis (modal + random) is required for R2. Thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver a STEP AP242 solid body plus a structural report with max von Mises, first natural frequency, total mass, first buckling load factor, and rail flatness.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
This design is delivered as a multi-part assembly rather than a single solid body. The decomposition comprises (A) deployer_rail_corners (x4), (B) side_panels_X (x2), (C) side_panels_Y (x2), (D) deck_plate_minus_Z, (E) deck_plate_plus_Z, (F) pc104_stack_rods (x4), (G) payload_mounting_tray, (H) adcs_module_plate. The final FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED model only — per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) must be <= 1.0 under the governing load case. All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria are added.
short_prompt:
Design a 3U CubeSat primary structure benchmark compliant with Cal Poly CubeSat Design Specification (CDS) Rev 14 for 3U deployers.

The structure is a monolithic 6061-T6 aluminum skeletonized chassis with external envelope 100.0 +/- 0.1 mm x 100.0 +/- 0.1 mm x 340.5 +/- 0.3 mm, four deployer rails on the long corners (8.5 x 8.5 mm rails, at least 85% of each 340.5 mm long rail in contact with the P-POD), and a total mass at or below 4.0 kg per CDS Rev 14 3U mass cap.

Material 6061-T6 (yield 276 MPa, ultimate 310 MPa, E 69 GPa, density 2700 kg/m^3).

Load cases per GEVS-SE GSFC-STD-7000: LC1 quasi-static 12 g axial + 7 g lateral simultaneously; LC2 sine vibration 2.5 g at 5-100 Hz; LC3 random vibration GRMS 14.1 at 20-2000 Hz; LC4 shock response 1000 g peak 100-10000 Hz.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: CDS Rev 14 envelope/rail/mass compliance is a rule-checklist on the geometry, not FEA.

prompt.title: CDS Rev 14 3U CubeSat primary structure (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Design a 6061-T6 3U CubeSat chassis (100 x 100 x 340.5 mm, <=4.0 kg) compliant with Cal Poly CDS Rev 14, passing GEVS quasi-static, modal >=100 Hz, and 2x buckling margin. [Multi-part assembly] deployer_rail_corners (x4), side_panels_X (x2), side_panels_Y (x2), deck_plate_minus_Z (x1), deck_plate_plus_Z (x1), pc104_stack_rods (x4), payload_mounting_tray (x1), adcs_module_plate (x1). FEM verification at assembled level only; connection DCR <= 1.0 under governing load case.

boundary_conditions.deployer_rails: constrained in P-POD by 4 rails with 85% contact

parts.name: deployer_rail_corners
parts.role: four long corner rails that contact the P-POD for 340.5 mm with 85% min contact; CDS Rev 14 rail-flatness body
parts.material_hint: 6061-T6 machined rail, yield 276 MPa, hard-anodized slip surface

parts.name: side_panels_X
parts.role: +X and -X side panels closing the X faces, attaching to rail corners and deck plates

parts.name: side_panels_Y
parts.role: +Y and -Y side panels closing the Y faces, attaching to rail corners and deck plates

parts.name: deck_plate_minus_Z
parts.role: -Z deck plate closing the bottom of the 3U tube and tying rail feet

parts.name: deck_plate_plus_Z
parts.role: +Z deck plate closing the top of the 3U tube and hosting antenna/ADCS interface

parts.name: pc104_stack_rods
parts.role: four threaded rods clamping the PC/104 board stack along Z (PC/104 85.60 x 82.55 mm mounting pattern)
parts.material_hint: A2-70 or A4-70 stainless threaded rods with nyloc nuts

parts.name: payload_mounting_tray
parts.role: internal tray carrying mission payload within one 1U cell of the 3U envelope

parts.name: adcs_module_plate
parts.role: plate mounting attitude determination and control module (reaction wheels or magnetorquers) within the bus
parts.material_hint: 6061-T6 plate with non-magnetic ADCS mounting bosses

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Al 6061-T6 yield 276 / SF 2.0 per NASA-STD-5001B Category A
Cal Poly CDS Rev 14 rail tolerance

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection DCR across all inter-part interfaces (rail-panel bolts, panel-deck bolts, PC/104 stack rod clamp, tray/ADCS mounts) <= 1.0 under LC1 + LC3.
Assembled structure must retain deployer rail 85% contact with P-POD after LC1 quasi-static deformation.

interfaces.load_path: deployer rail corners A are bolted to +/-X side panels B along full 340.5 mm length; transfer shear between rails and panels
deployer rail corners A are bolted to +/-Y side panels C; provides torsional closure
rail corners A bolt to -Z deck plate D at bottom; transfers axial launch load into deck
rail corners A bolt to +Z deck plate E at top; closes tube at separation face
PC/104 stack rods F are threaded into -Z deck plate D bosses; preload clamps the board stack
PC/104 stack rods F thread into +Z deck plate E nuts; completes stack preload chain
payload mounting tray G is clamped between stack rods F via standoffs in PC/104 pattern
ADCS module plate H is clamped between stack rods F via standoffs in PC/104 pattern

fem_check_policy.rationale: First natural frequency, launch-load stress, buckling margin, and deployer rail flatness depend on the assembled structure (rail-panel-deck stiffness + PC/104 stack clamp preload); per-part metrics are reported but do not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: Although the original sample is described as monolithic, real CubeSat primary structures are invariably bolted assemblies of rails + panels + deck plates + PC/104 stack rods; multi-part FEA captures the realistic stiffness + joint margins.
notes.speculation_caveats: parts[] counts and interfaces[] reflect typical CDS Rev 14 3U compliant skeletonized chassis designs; not derived from a specific UK SEDS SDC rulebook.
